name: WBC
description: Total count of immune-system cells circulating in the blood. Elevated counts can point to active infection, inflammation, or hematologic disease; low counts can indicate immunosuppression, marrow problems, or post-viral states.

name: RBC
description: Number of red blood cells per unit volume. Low counts contribute to anemia and reduced oxygen delivery; high counts can reflect dehydration, smoking, sleep apnea, or polycythemia.

name: Hemoglobin
description: The iron-containing protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen. Trends matter as much as the absolute value — a steady decline can be an early signal of iron deficiency, bleeding, or chronic disease.

name: Hematocrit
description: Percentage of blood volume occupied by red blood cells. Moves in tandem with hemoglobin and RBC; the three together help characterize anemia or polycythemia.

name: MCV
description: Average red blood cell size. Microcytic (low) suggests iron deficiency or thalassemia; macrocytic (high) points toward B12/folate deficiency or alcohol use.

name: MCH
description: Average hemoglobin content per red blood cell. Tracks with MCV and helps narrow the cause of anemia.

name: MCHC
description: Average hemoglobin concentration inside a red blood cell, independent of cell size. Low values can reflect iron-deficiency anemia; high values are rare and seen in spherocytosis.

name: RDW
description: Variation in red blood cell size. Elevated RDW with a normal MCV is an early sign of iron, B12, or folate deficiency before the average cell size shifts.

name: Platelets
description: Cell fragments responsible for blood clotting. Low platelets (thrombocytopenia) increase bleeding risk; high platelets can reflect inflammation, iron deficiency, or marrow disorders.

name: MPV
description: Average size of platelets. Higher values suggest younger, more reactive platelets — useful when interpreting platelet count changes.

name: Neutrophils
description: Frontline white blood cells against bacterial infection. The percentage rises with acute bacterial infection and stress; falls with viral infection or marrow suppression.

name: Lymphocytes
description: White blood cells that drive viral immunity and antibody production. Elevated in viral illness; suppressed in some chronic infections, corticosteroid use, and immune disorders.

name: Monocytes
description: Cleanup cells that mature into macrophages. Persistently elevated values can reflect chronic inflammation or recovery from infection.

name: Eosinophils
description: Allergy and parasite-fighting cells. Elevated values are seen with allergies, asthma, parasitic infections, and some drug reactions.

name: Basophils
description: Rare white blood cells involved in allergic and inflammatory responses. Persistent elevation is uncommon and can warrant follow-up for myeloproliferative disorders.

name: Glucose
description: Blood sugar level after at least 8 hours of fasting. Values 100–125 mg/dL indicate prediabetes; ≥126 on two occasions meets the diabetes threshold. Pair with HbA1c for trend context.

name: BUN
description: A nitrogen waste product from protein metabolism, cleared by the kidneys. Rises with dehydration, kidney impairment, high protein intake, or GI bleeding.

name: Creatinine
description: Muscle waste cleared by the kidneys; the most-used marker of kidney function. Higher in people with more muscle mass — interpret alongside eGFR rather than as an absolute.

name: eGFR
description: Estimated kidney filtration rate, calculated from creatinine, age, and sex. ≥60 is considered normal; sustained values below 60 over three months meet the definition of chronic kidney disease.

name: Sodium
description: Primary extracellular electrolyte. Reflects water balance more than salt intake — abnormalities usually point to hydration status, kidney function, or hormonal regulation.

name: Potassium
description: Critical for heart rhythm and muscle function. Both high and low values can cause arrhythmias; common drivers include diuretics, kidney disease, and ACE inhibitors.

name: Chloride
description: Tracks closely with sodium. Useful for evaluating acid-base balance — abnormal values can indicate metabolic acidosis or alkalosis.

name: CO2
description: Serum bicarbonate level — the kidney-side buffer in acid-base balance. Low values suggest metabolic acidosis; high values, metabolic alkalosis or respiratory compensation.

name: Calcium
description: Total serum calcium. Tightly regulated by parathyroid hormone and vitamin D; persistent abnormalities warrant evaluation of those pathways.

name: Total_Protein
description: Sum of albumin and globulin. Low values can reflect malnutrition or liver disease; high values can suggest chronic inflammation or paraprotein disorders.

name: Albumin
description: The main protein made by the liver. Falls with malnutrition, chronic disease, and liver or kidney problems; a robust marker of overall protein status.

name: Globulin
description: Calculated as total protein minus albumin. Elevated globulin can reflect chronic inflammation or immune activation.

name: AG_Ratio
description: Albumin-to-globulin ratio. Persistently low values can warrant follow-up for chronic inflammation or paraprotein disorders.

name: Bilirubin_Total
description: Breakdown product of red blood cells, processed by the liver. Elevated values point to liver/biliary issues or accelerated red cell turnover; Gilbert's syndrome is a benign cause of mild elevations.

name: ALP
description: Enzyme present in liver, bile ducts, and bone. Elevations are interpreted by which tissue source dominates — biliary obstruction vs bone turnover.

name: ALT
description: Liver-specific enzyme released when hepatocytes are damaged. The most sensitive routine marker for liver injury (alcohol, viral hepatitis, NAFLD, medications).

name: AST
description: Liver and muscle enzyme. Interpreted alongside ALT — an AST:ALT ratio >2 suggests alcohol-related liver injury, while a low ratio is more typical of NAFLD or viral hepatitis.

name: GGT
description: Liver / biliary enzyme. Sensitive but nonspecific — elevated with alcohol use, NAFLD, biliary disease, and many medications.

name: Cholesterol_Total
description: Sum of all cholesterol fractions. A blunt instrument by itself — LDL, HDL, and triglycerides give the real risk picture.

name: LDL
description: "Bad" cholesterol — the strongest routine lipid predictor of atherosclerotic cardiovascular risk. Targets vary by overall risk; below 100 mg/dL is a common goal for adults without elevated risk.

name: HDL
description: "Good" cholesterol that shuttles lipids back to the liver. Higher values are generally protective; extremely high values aren't always beneficial.

name: Triglycerides
description: Circulating fat from diet and liver synthesis. Driven heavily by recent meals, alcohol, and refined carbs; fast >9 hours for an accurate reading.

name: VLDL
description: Triglyceride-rich lipoprotein, usually estimated as triglycerides ÷ 5. Tracks closely with triglycerides.

name: Cholesterol_HDL_Ratio
description: Total cholesterol divided by HDL. A rough single-number indicator of atherosclerotic risk; lower is better.

name: TSH
description: Pituitary signal that drives thyroid hormone production. The most sensitive screening test for thyroid function — high TSH suggests hypothyroidism, low TSH suggests hyperthyroidism.

name: Free_T3
description: Active thyroid hormone. Useful for confirming thyroid status when TSH and free T4 are equivocal or when hyperthyroidism is suspected.

name: Free_T4
description: Primary thyroid hormone in circulation. Paired with TSH it characterizes whether thyroid dysfunction is primary (thyroid gland) or central (pituitary).

name: Iron
description: Serum iron level. Varies significantly through the day and with recent meals; interpret alongside ferritin and iron saturation.

name: TIBC
description: How much iron the blood can carry. Rises in iron deficiency and falls in chronic disease or iron overload.

name: Ferritin
description: Iron storage protein and the best single marker of iron stores. Also an acute-phase reactant — values can appear normal in iron deficiency during active inflammation.

name: Iron_Saturation
description: Percentage of transferrin that is bound to iron. Low values support iron deficiency; high values (>45%) raise concern for iron overload / hemochromatosis.

name: Vitamin_D
description: The main circulating form of vitamin D and the best marker of vitamin D status. Affects bone health, immune function, and many tissues; insufficiency is common in northern climates.

name: Vitamin_B12
description: Essential for red blood cell production and neurological function. Deficiency is common in vegetarians and older adults; can present with macrocytic anemia or neuropathy before becoming obvious.

name: Folate
description: B-vitamin essential for DNA synthesis. Deficiency causes macrocytic anemia and is important to identify in early pregnancy.

name: CRP
description: Liver-made acute-phase protein that rises with inflammation. The high-sensitivity assay (hs-CRP) is used to assess cardiovascular risk; persistently elevated values warrant investigation.

name: ESR
description: How fast red blood cells settle in a tube — a slow-moving indicator of inflammation. Rises and falls more gradually than CRP and is useful for tracking chronic inflammatory disease.

name: Homocysteine
description: Amino acid metabolite. Elevated levels are associated with cardiovascular disease and cognitive decline; often respond to B12, B6, and folate supplementation.

name: Testosterone_Total
description: Total circulating testosterone, mostly bound to SHBG. Most reliable in the morning when levels peak; persistent low values with symptoms warrant evaluation.

name: Testosterone_Free
description: The biologically active fraction of testosterone, unbound to SHBG. Sometimes lower than expected even when total testosterone is normal.

name: Estradiol
description: Primary estrogen in circulation. In premenopausal women it varies dramatically across the menstrual cycle; in men, persistent elevations can affect libido and gynecomastia risk.

name: DHEA_S
description: Adrenal androgen precursor that declines steadily with age. A marker of adrenal function; high values warrant evaluation for adrenal hyperfunction.

name: Cortisol
description: Stress hormone with a strong daily rhythm — highest in the morning, lowest at night. A single value is interpretable only when timing and context are known.

name: HbA1c
description: Average blood glucose over the last ~3 months, expressed as percentage of glycated hemoglobin. 5.7–6.4% indicates prediabetes; ≥6.5% meets the diabetes threshold.

name: Insulin
description: Fasting insulin level. Combined with fasting glucose to compute HOMA-IR, an early signal of insulin resistance that can predate elevated glucose by years.

name: PSA
description: Prostate-specific protein. Elevated levels can reflect benign enlargement, prostatitis, or cancer; trends and PSA velocity are more useful than a single value.

name: Uric_Acid
description: End product of purine metabolism. Elevated values are associated with gout, kidney stones, and metabolic syndrome; modulated by diet (purines, fructose) and kidney clearance.
